en: But reckon up in how many directions we are met by unsatisfied longings; the denial of procreation and begetting, for which purpose nature has endowed us with great energy; the unending monotony of our highly developed modern methods of "division of labour," which excludes any interest in the work itself; and above all our effortless security against war, lawlessness, robbery, epidemics, infant and woman mortality—all this gives a sum of surplus energy which must needs find an outlet. But how? A relatively few create quasi-natural dangers for themselves in reckless sport; many more, seeking to find some equivalent for their more primitive energy, take to alcoholic excess; others expend themselves in the rush of money-making, or in the morbid performance of duties, in perpetual over-work. By such means they try to escape a dangerous storing-up of energy which might force mad outlets for itself. It is for such reasons that we have to-day a sexual question . It is in this direction that men's energy would like to expend itself as it has done from time immemorial in periods of security and abundance. Under such circumstances it is not only rabbits that multiply; men and women, too, become the sport of these accesses of nature: the sport, because their moral views have confined them in a narrow cage, the excessive narrowness of which was not felt so long as harsh external necessity pressed upon them with even greater constraint. But now the man of the cities finds the space too circumscribed. He is surrounded by alluring temptation, and like an invisible procureur there slinks through society the knowledge of preventive methods which evade all consequences. Why then moral restraint? Out of religious consideration for an angry God? Apart from the prevalence of widespread unbelief, even the believing man might quietly ask himself whether, if he himself were God, he would punish the youthful erotic uncontrol of John and Mary with twice twenty-four years of imprisonment and seething in boiling oil. Such ideas are no longer compatible with our decorous conception of God. The God of our time is necessarily much too tolerant to make a great fuss over it; (knavishness and hypocrisy are a thousand times worse). In this way the somewhat ascetic and hypocritical sexual morality of our time has had the ground cut from under its feet. Or is it the case that we are now protected from dissoluteness by superior wisdom, recognition of the nothingness of human happenings? Unfortunately we are very far from that; rather does the hypnotic power of tradition keep us in bonds, and through cowardice and thoughtlessness and habit the herd goes tramping on in this same path. But man possesses in the unconscious a fine scent for the spirit of his time; he has an inkling of his own possibilities and he feels in his innermost heart the instability of the foundations of present-day morality, no longer supported by living religious conviction. It is thus the greater number of the erotic conflicts of our time originate. Instinct thirsting for liberty thrusts itself up against the yielding barriers of morality: men are tempted, they desire and do not desire. And because they will not and cannot think out to its logical conclusion what it is they really desire, their erotic conflict is largely unconscious; whence comes neurosis. Neurosis then is most intimately bound up with the problem of our times and represents an unsuccessful attempt of the individual to solve the general problem in his own person. Neurosis is a tearing in two of the inner self . For most men the reason of this cleavage is the fact that their conscious self desires to hold to its moral ideal, whilst the unconscious strives after the amoral ideal, steadfastly rejected by the conscious self. People of this kind would like to appear more decent than they really are. But the conflict is often of an opposite kind. There are those who do not outwardly live a decent life at all and do not place the slightest constraint upon their sexuality, but in reality this is a sinful pose assumed for goodness knows what reasons, for down below they have a decorous soul which has somehow gone astray in their unconscious, just as has the real immoral nature in the case of apparently moral people. Extremes of conduct always arouse suspicions of the opposite tendencies in the unconscious.
zh: 請計算一下，我們在多少方面遭遇了未獲滿足的渴望：對生育與繁衍的否定——而自然為此賦予了我們巨大的能量；我們高度發達的現代「分工」方法所帶來的無盡單調，它排除了對工作本身的任何興趣；以及最重要的，我們不費吹灰之力就享有的免於戰爭、違法、搶劫、流行病、嬰兒與婦女死亡的安全——這一切加總起來，形成了一股必須找到出口的剩餘能量。但如何找到呢？相對少數的人在魯莽的運動中為自己製造了準自然的危險；更多的人則試圖為自己更原始的能量尋找某種替代品，於是沉溺於酒精過量；另一些人則在追逐金錢的匆忙中，或在病態地履行職責、持續過度工作中耗盡自己。他們藉此試圖逃離一種危險的能量積蓄，這種積蓄可能會迫使能量以瘋狂的方式宣洩。正是基於這些原因，我們今天才有所謂的性問題。正是在這個方向上，人的能量想要耗費自身，正如自遠古以來，在安全與富足的時期，它一直所做的那樣。在這種情況下，不僅是兔子會大量繁殖；男人和女人也成了這種自然衝動的玩物：之所以說是玩物，是因為他們的道德觀念將他們限制在一個狹窄的籠子裡，而這個籠子過度的狹窄，在嚴酷的外部必然性以更大的約束力壓迫他們時，他們並未感受到。但如今，城市裡的人覺得這個空間過於侷促。他被誘人的試探所包圍，而關於能規避一切後果的預防方法的知識，像一個看不見的拉皮條者，潛行於社會之中。那麼，為何還要道德約束呢？出於對一位憤怒之神的宗教顧慮嗎？除了普遍的不信神之外，即使是有信仰的人，也可能會靜靜地捫心自問：如果他自己是神，他是否會因為約翰和瑪麗年輕時無法控制的色慾，就懲罰他們被監禁四十八年並在滾油中煎熬？這樣的想法，已不再與我們對神體面的觀念相容。我們時代的神，必然過於寬容，不會對此大驚小怪（奸詐與偽善要糟糕一千倍）。這樣一來，我們這個時代那帶有幾分禁慾與偽善的性道德，其根基已被抽掉。或者，我們現在是憑藉更高的智慧、對人類事件虛無性的認識，才得以免受放蕩的侵害？不幸的是，我們離此還很遙遠；相反，傳統的催眠力量將我們束縛其中，而由於怯懦、不假思索與習慣，羊群繼續沿著同一條路踐踏前行。但人在無意識中，對其時代的精神有著敏銳的嗅覺；他隱約察覺到自己的可能性，並在內心深處感受到當今道德基礎的不穩定——這些基礎已不再有活生生的宗教信念支撐。我們時代大部分的性愛衝突，正是由此產生。渴望自由的本能，衝撞著正在退讓的道德藩籬：人被試探，他們既渴望又不渴望。而因為他們不願、也無法將自己真正渴望的東西思考到其邏輯結論，他們的性愛衝突在很大程度上是無意識的；神經症由此而來。因此，神經症與我們時代的問題有著最緊密的聯繫，它代表個體試圖在自己身上解決這個普遍問題的一次失敗嘗試。神經症是內在自我的分裂。對大多數人來說，這種分裂的原因在於，他們的意識自我想要堅守其道德理想，而無意識則奮力追求那被意識自我堅決拒絕的非道德理想。這類人希望自己看起來比實際更體面。但衝突常常是相反類型的。有些人外在根本不過體面的生活，對自己的性慾絲毫不加約束，但實際上，這是一種出於天知道什麼原因而採取的罪惡姿態，因為在他們內心深處，有一個體面的靈魂，不知何故在他們的無意識中迷失了方向，正如在表面道德的人身上，真實的不道德本性也迷失了一樣。行為的極端總是引發對其無意識中相反傾向的懷疑。
